title: British Monarchs
description: Kings and queens of England and Great Britain

cards.front: William the Conqueror (1066–1087)
cards.back: First Norman king of England; won the Battle of Hastings in 1066 and commissioned the Domesday Book

cards.front: Who won the Battle of Hastings in 1066 and became the first Norman king of England?
cards.back: William the Conqueror
First Plantagenet king; expanded royal courts and clashed with Thomas Becket, Archbishop of Canterbury, who was murdered in Canterbury Cathedral

cards.front: Which English king's conflict with Thomas Becket led to Becket's murder in Canterbury Cathedral?
cards.back: Henry II

cards.front: Richard I, "the Lionheart" (1189–1199)
cards.back: Famous warrior king who led the Third Crusade; spent most of his reign abroad fighting and was later held for ransom in Austria

cards.front: Which English king led the Third Crusade and was known as "the Lionheart"?
cards.back: Richard I

cards.front: King John (1199–1216)
cards.back: Forced by rebel barons to sign the Magna Carta in 1215, limiting royal power and establishing the principle that the king is subject to law

cards.front: Which English king was forced to sign the Magna Carta in 1215?
cards.back: King John

cards.front: What document was signed in 1215, limiting the power of the English monarch?
cards.back: Magna Carta (signed by King John)
Led England to a famous victory at the Battle of Agincourt (1415) during the Hundred Years' War; subject of a Shakespeare play

cards.front: Which English king won the Battle of Agincourt in 1415?
cards.back: Henry V

cards.front: Henry VIII (1509–1547)
cards.back: Had six wives; broke with the Catholic Church and established the Church of England (Anglican Church) so he could annul his marriage to Catherine of Aragon

cards.front: Which English king established the Church of England after breaking with the Pope?
cards.back: Henry VIII

cards.front: How many wives did Henry VIII have?
cards.back: Six: Catherine of Aragon, Anne Boleyn, Jane Seymour, Anne of Cleves, Catherine Howard, Catherine Parr

cards.front: Henry VIII's 1st wife
cards.back: Catherine of Aragon (divorced; mother of Mary I)

cards.front: Henry VIII's 2nd wife
cards.back: Anne Boleyn (beheaded; mother of Elizabeth I)

cards.front: Henry VIII's 3rd wife
cards.back: Jane Seymour (died after childbirth; mother of Edward VI)

cards.front: Henry VIII's 4th wife
cards.back: Anne of Cleves (divorced; marriage annulled after six months)

cards.front: Henry VIII's 5th wife
cards.back: Catherine Howard (beheaded for alleged adultery)

cards.front: Henry VIII's 6th wife
cards.back: Catherine Parr (survived Henry VIII; helped reconcile him with his daughters)

cards.front: Which wife of Henry VIII was the mother of Elizabeth I?
cards.back: Anne Boleyn (2nd wife; beheaded in 1536)

cards.front: Which wife of Henry VIII was the mother of Edward VI?
cards.back: Jane Seymour (3rd wife; died shortly after childbirth)

cards.front: Mnemonic for the fates of Henry VIII's six wives
cards.back: Divorced, beheaded, died, divorced, beheaded, survived

cards.front: Edward VI (1547–1553)
cards.back: Son of Henry VIII and Jane Seymour; became king at age 9 and strengthened Protestantism in England; died at 15

cards.front: Which son of Henry VIII became king at age 9 and promoted Protestantism?
cards.back: Edward VI
Daughter of Henry VIII and Catherine of Aragon; restored Catholicism and earned the nickname "Bloody Mary" for burning Protestant heretics

cards.front: Which English queen was nicknamed "Bloody Mary"?
cards.back: Mary I (daughter of Henry VIII and Catherine of Aragon)

cards.front: Elizabeth I (1558–1603)
cards.back: The "Virgin Queen"; defeated the Spanish Armada in 1588, presided over the Elizabethan era of exploration and Shakespeare, and never married

cards.front: Which English monarch defeated the Spanish Armada in 1588?
cards.back: Elizabeth I

cards.front: Why is Elizabeth I called the "Virgin Queen"?
cards.back: She never married and had no children; the colony of Virginia was named in her honor

cards.front: James I of England / James VI of Scotland (1603–1625)
cards.back: First Stuart king of England; united the English and Scottish crowns, authorized the King James Bible (1611), and survived the Gunpowder Plot (1605)

cards.front: Which English king authorized the King James Bible in 1611?
cards.back: James I (James VI of Scotland)

cards.front: What was the Gunpowder Plot of 1605?
cards.back: A failed Catholic conspiracy led by Guy Fawkes to blow up Parliament and kill King James I

cards.front: Charles I (1625–1649)
cards.back: His conflicts with Parliament led to the English Civil War; he was defeated by Oliver Cromwell's forces and beheaded in 1649

cards.front: Which English king was executed (beheaded) in 1649 after the English Civil War?
cards.back: Charles I

cards.front: Who led the Parliamentary forces that defeated Charles I in the English Civil War?
cards.back: Oliver Cromwell

cards.front: Charles II (1660–1685)
cards.back: Known as the "Merry Monarch"; restored the monarchy in 1660 after the Commonwealth period (the Restoration)

cards.front: Which English king was restored to the throne in 1660, ending the Commonwealth?
cards.back: Charles II (the Restoration)

cards.front: William III & Mary II (1689–1702/1694)
cards.back: Came to power in the Glorious Revolution of 1688; signed the English Bill of Rights (1689), establishing constitutional monarchy

cards.front: What was the Glorious Revolution of 1688?
cards.back: The bloodless overthrow of James II, replaced by William III and Mary II, who accepted the English Bill of Rights

cards.front: Queen Anne (1702–1714)
cards.back: Last Stuart monarch; oversaw the Acts of Union (1707) that united England and Scotland into the Kingdom of Great Britain

cards.front: Which queen was the last Stuart monarch and oversaw the union of England and Scotland in 1707?
cards.back: Queen Anne

cards.front: George III (1760–1820)
cards.back: King during the American Revolution; lost the American colonies; later suffered from periods of mental illness

cards.front: Which British king ruled during the American Revolution and lost the American colonies?
cards.back: George III

cards.front: Queen Victoria (1837–1901)
cards.back: Reigned for 63 years during the height of the British Empire; the Victorian era saw industrialization, expansion, and cultural change; longest-reigning monarch until Elizabeth II

cards.front: Which British monarch gives her name to the era of industrialization and imperial expansion in the 1800s?
cards.back: Queen Victoria (reigned 1837–1901)

cards.front: Edward VII (1901–1910)
cards.back: Son of Queen Victoria; his reign is called the Edwardian era; helped modernize the British military and improve relations with France

cards.front: Which British king's short reign (1901–1910) is known as the Edwardian era?
cards.back: Edward VII (son of Queen Victoria)
Reigned during World War I; changed the royal family's name from Saxe-Coburg-Gotha to Windsor in 1917 due to anti-German sentiment

cards.front: Which British king changed the royal family's name to Windsor during World War I?
cards.back: George V (in 1917, from Saxe-Coburg-Gotha)

cards.front: George VI (1936–1952)
cards.back: Became king after his brother Edward VIII abdicated; led Britain through World War II alongside Winston Churchill; father of Elizabeth II

cards.front: Which British king led the country through World War II and was the father of Elizabeth II?
cards.back: George VI

cards.front: Why did Edward VIII abdicate in 1936?
cards.back: He chose to marry Wallis Simpson, an American divorcee, which was unacceptable to the Church of England and the government

cards.front: Elizabeth II (1952–2022)
cards.back: Longest-reigning British monarch at 70 years; oversaw decolonization, the end of the Cold War, and the modernization of the monarchy

cards.front: Who is the longest-reigning British monarch, serving for 70 years?
cards.back: Elizabeth II (1952–2022)

cards.front: Charles III (2022–present)
cards.back: Became king at age 73 upon the death of his mother, Elizabeth II; previously the longest-serving Prince of Wales

cards.front: Who became king of the United Kingdom in 2022?
cards.back: Charles III
